summary: qr-code-styling-temp exfiltrates host identifiers via DNS to oob.sl4x0.xyz at install and require
details: Package name impersonates the popular qr-code-styling library but ships no QR functionality. On `npm install` (via package.json scripts.install running `node index.js`) and again whenever the module is required, lib/core.js reads `os.userInfo().username`, `os.hostname()`, and `process.cwd()`, joins them with a timestamp and a 'samsung' tag, and issues a `dns.resolve4` lookup against `<encoded-host-data>.oob.sl4x0.xyz`. The DNS channel is chosen to bypass HTTP egress controls. The module/API names ('os','dns','process','userInfo','hostname','cwd','resolve4') and the C2 domain 'oob.sl4x0.xyz' are obfuscated as charCode arrays in lib/b02e30.js and lib/6ad264.js (e.g., `[0x6f,0x6f,0x62,0x2e,0x73,0x6c,0x34,0x78,0x30,0x2e,0x78,0x79,0x7a]` → 'oob.sl4x0.xyz') decoded at runtime. The combination of typosquat name, decoy 'enterprise utilities' description, install-time + require-time host-identifier exfiltration over DNS, and string obfuscation specifically hiding the C2 leaves no benign interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
